title: Les mystères de Paris, Tome III
summaries: "Les mystères de Paris, Tome III" by Eugène Sue is a serial novel published between 1842 and 1843. This groundbreaking work follows the mysterious Rodolphe as he seeks to restore social justice in nineteenth-century Paris, encountering workers, criminals, and the city's poorest residents. What began as a tale of the "dangerous classes" evolved into pioneering social literature of the underworld, as Sue received thousands of letters from readers and transformed his story into a platform addressing prison conditions, hospital care, and the cost of justice.
